name: Reconcile the canonical mobile flow inventory and E2E manifests with the V2 shell
description: docs/flows/mobile-app-flow-inventory.md is the canonical mobile journey inventory, but its roughly 280 rows still center V0/V1 tab and mode behavior, older routes, and legacy test assumptions while the latest preview enables EXPO_PUBLIC_ENABLE_MODE_NAV_V2 and presents the Mentor, Subjects, Journal, and Account shell with learner/supporter person-scope chips. The inventory and its cited Playwright/Maestro/Jest manifests therefore no longer give QA a reliable map of what is current, flag-specific legacy insurance, superseded, or actually covered. This is a documentation and test-governance defect: stale canonical rows can direct reviewers through the wrong information architecture and make missing V2 journey coverage appear present.
acceptanceCriteria:
- Audit every row in docs/flows/mobile-app-flow-inventory.md against the latest V2 preview flags, current routes/navigation contracts, the V2 shell specification, and maintained tests.
- Add or update the V2 Mentor, Subjects, Journal, and Account journeys, including learner/supporter scope chips, person selection, scope-preserving tab changes, landing routes, back/exit behavior, and visibility/authorization boundaries.
- Retain required V0/V1 regression insurance, but label each legacy row explicitly as flag-specific current coverage, superseded behavior, or historical reference; do not erase supported legacy flag-state coverage.
- Reconcile every cited Playwright, Maestro, and Jest manifest/path/assertion with the maintained files and the build profile in which it can run; remove or mark claims that do not prove the described flow.
- The navigation-shell matrix states the checked environment/profile and records V2 alongside V0/V1 without calling any mutable flag state a universal default.
- Each active row has a traceable source/spec owner and evidence status, and contradictory duplicate rows are resolved or cross-linked.
- Add a regression check, where practical, for stale referenced test files/routes and inventory rows whose declared flag/shell state no longer matches the canonical navigation contract.
- Review confirms the refreshed inventory can be used to plan both Chromium and native-device QA without silently substituting V0/V1 flows for V2.
riskImpact: P2: the canonical QA inventory can send testers through obsolete routes and overstate regression coverage, allowing V2 navigation, person-scope, consent, and recovery defects to escape while preserving no clear record of which V0/V1 checks remain intentional.
bodyText:
Acceptance Criteria (PM-ratified 2026-07-19, Opus-drafted, grounded at main 85629757c)
Risk/Impact: P2 QA-governance defect — docs/flows/mobile-app-flow-inventory.md (~280 rows) still centers V0/V1 IA with drifted code cites; it can route reviewers through obsolete routes and make missing V2 coverage look present. Evidence class: reconciled doc/manifest diffs + a consistency check — not product tests.
AC-1 (matrix gains V2 column): The 'Navigation shell matrix' (mobile-app-flow-inventory.md:34) adds a V2 (MODE_NAV_V2_ENABLED) column for the Mentor/Subjects/Journal + Account shell, and every cell agrees with apps/mobile/src/lib/navigation-contract.ts (+ legacy-navigation-contract.ts, feature-flags.ts). Evidence: the reviewed matrix diff.
AC-2 (fix drifted cites): The matrix's 'Mode semantics' line citations are corrected against the current 643-line navigation-contract.ts — present cites :266-315 / :146-151 / :390-437 / :475-478 are stale (actual: STUDY_TABS :153-158, V2_ROUTES :178, canEnter :457-527). Evidence: diff showing each cite resolves to the asserted symbol.
AC-3 (V2 journeys added): New/updated rows cover V2 Mentor/Subjects/Journal/Account journeys incl. learner/supporter person-scope chips, person selection, scope-preserving tab changes, landing routes, back/exit, and visibility/authorization boundaries. Evidence: added rows with route/spec cites.
AC-4 (legacy insurance labeled, not erased): Every retained V0/V1 row is tagged flag-specific-current vs superseded vs historical (extend the Status legend :20-32 with a V2 tag); no supported legacy flag-state coverage is deleted. Evidence: legend + tagged rows.
AC-5 (manifest reconciliation): Every cited Playwright (apps/mobile/e2e-web/flows/**), Maestro (apps/mobile/e2e/flows/**/*.yaml), and Jest manifest/path/assertion is reconciled with the maintained file and the build profile it runs under; claims that don't prove the described flow are removed or marked. Evidence: per-manifest reconciliation table (path exists / runs-under-profile / proves-flow).
AC-6 (no universal default): The matrix states the checked environment/profile and records V2 alongside V0/V1 without labeling any flag state a universal default (AGENTS.md: nav mode is per-environment). Evidence: reviewed matrix wording.
AC-7 (consistency check, where practical): A lightweight guard flags inventory rows whose declared flag/shell state no longer matches the canonical navigation contract, and stale referenced test files/routes. Evidence: a scripts/check-*.ts following repo precedent (check-decision-adr-link.ts, check-i18n-keep-rot.ts) wired into docs-checks.yml, passing on the reconciled doc.
Refinement notes: Effort=M (docs+governance; no product code). e2e-web smoke is not evidence for any layout claim added to rows — mark full-spec vs smoke per row. Keep AC-7 a cite-rot guard, not a new harness.
